codeReview.summary: Reviewed fix commit 5b4dbefa739cbb1b4183176ab5185c47fc58a527 against prior failing commit 93314c577ce0202b38b77cdde30b98a9d506fde0 and prior review findings. The fix directly addresses both blocking concerns: it adds epsilon-safe cost-only drift guarding in the production reconciliation path (`missingCost > 1e-9`) and replaces the prior hand-computed idempotency coverage with tests that execute `computeSupplementalUsages` and assert persisted post-insert convergence on rerun.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker handoff marks `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but transcript evidence does not show the required reads from `AGENTS.md`, `.factory/library/architecture.md`, and `.factory/library/user-testing.md` mandated by the token-accounting-worker procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session `b8ac495a-581c-4a6b-9e17-af8914b5749e` shows reads of `mission.md`, `validation-contract.md`, and `features.json`, but no reads of the three required files listed in `.factory/skills/token-accounting-worker/SKILL.md` step 1.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared library guidance appears mission-misaligned, which can reduce startup efficiency for token-accounting/reconciliation workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md` and `.factory/library/user-testing.md` document multi-account switcher/browser/CLI validation flows, while mission `76dfbf36-62ab-43ad-899f-b8b8f9e47932` is focused on exact-first token accounting and reconciliation/backfill hardening.

summary: Pass. The epsilon reconciliation fix resolves the prior blocking precision bug and strengthens idempotency validation through real reconciliation-pipeline execution with persistence-aware assertions; no new blocking code issues were found in the reviewed diff.
